bullets.text: Combined ratio of 92.6 was strong and in line with seasonal factors; investment portfolio achieved a 5.5% total return for Q2, contributing to the most acceptable quarter in some time.

Capital position surpassed prior-year levels and was comfortably more than $1 billion above regulatory capital plus extreme contingency load; portfolio risk profile significantly improved from a year earlier.
Personal Lines policies in force grew 4% year-over-year, approaching 11 million policies; Direct auto written premium grew 9% for both the quarter and year-to-date, driven by strong conversion rates.
Net premium growth remained in positive territory primarily through unit growth, as average premium per policy trends were still negative; premium growth remained too small for management's liking.
Agency channel showed its strongest new business sales in some time as competitors took rate ahead of Progressive, improving relative price competitiveness in agent comparison-rating systems.

Personal Injury Protection trends were described as an outlier of serious concern in several important states; management flagged adequacy of PIP pricing assumptions and was taking corrective rate actions.

Claims workforce was reduced by about 2% in Q2 while handling a larger customer base; policies-in-force per employee equivalent continued to improve with restructured claims processes at lower ongoing cost.
Name Your Price expanded nationwide during the quarter after nearly a year of test marketing; Commercial Auto showed signs of stabilizing at the bottom of its economic-induced decline.

Consumer demand remained elevated as economic conditions prompted cost-consciousness; management noted competitive positioning improving as some agency competitors needed rate increases in excess of loss-cost trends.
